text: An anecdote
Vivid
To recall
Eventful
Unexpected
A turning point
To set off
To run into
To get lost
A mishap
To end up
Anecdote
Out of the blue, we ran into an old friend.
I was walking along the beach when I met him.
While we were driving, the engine stopped.
When I was younger, I used to travel alone.
Every summer, we would drive to the beach.
Pricila, have I ever told you about the most eventful day of my last trip? We set off early for a quiet village.
No, tell me! What happened? I love a good anecdote.
Well, while we were driving up the mountain, the engine died, completely unexpected. Then we got lost on a tiny road.
Oh no, a real mishap! So what did you do then?
A farmer was passing by, and we ended up in his kitchen drinking coffee. Eventually that became the best part of the trip.
That's a real turning point! I still recall mine just as vividly.

Let me tell you about the most eventful day of my last trip. We had set off early for a quiet village in the mountains, expecting nothing special. While we were driving up the narrow road, the engine suddenly died, right out of the blue. So there we were, stuck, looking back at the valley below. An old farmer was passing by, and he offered to help. Eventually we ended up in his kitchen, drinking coffee, while he told us stories about the village. What looked like a disaster became the highlight of the whole journey. Sometimes the best anecdotes come from the moments that go wrong.

When I was younger, I used to travel very fast. I would visit five cities in a week and take hundreds of photos, but I could hardly recall a single moment afterward. Looking back now, I realize that I was collecting places, not stories. The real turning point came on a slow trip a few years ago. I got lost in a small town, and a stranger walked me home. That one unexpected hour stayed with me more vividly than entire fast holidays. So now I travel slowly, on purpose. I want to come home with stories worth telling, not just a longer list of stamps.

First, Pricila sets the scene: she was hiking alone in the mountains. Then, while she was walking, the weather suddenly changed. Next, out of the blue, she ran into another traveler on the trail. After that, they got lost together and had a small mishap with the map. Finally, they eventually found the village, and it became her favorite anecdote from the whole trip.
